meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your SaaS. Learn to build high-converting user onboarding with Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your MVP actually ships and generates revenue.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish projects' syndrome stems from a lack of user-facing feedback loops. By implementing structured, data-driven onboarding, you transform a feature-heavy slog into a clear path to value. This forces technical scope reduction, ensures rapid MVP validation, and keeps users paying while you iterate on your core product.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is filled with half-finished prototypes and abandoned GitHub repositories. You start with a vision of a transformative AI tool, but three weeks in, feature creep sets in, the architecture feels brittle, and your motivation evaporates. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a 'hook'—a validated onboarding experience that proves your value before you build the rest of the application.

True velocity isn't about writing code faster; it's about delivering the 'Aha!' moment to your user in under 60 seconds. When you focus on onboarding first, you are forced to define the absolute minimum viable path to value. This doesn't just improve retention; it provides the psychological closure needed to finish your build and launch to a paying audience.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Milestone
steps.description: Identify the one action that proves your user gets value. This is the only thing that matters for your MVP onboarding flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my core feature list and identify the single most impactful action a user can take. Suggest a 3-step onboarding flow that leads them to this action immediately.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Identify the core value milestone and outline the onboarding flow schema.'

steps.title: Implement Progressive Profiling
steps.description: Stop asking for everything upfront. Only capture data when it is strictly necessary to unlock the next feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a progressive onboarding form that captures user intent based on their initial interaction with the dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file ./src/components/OnboardingForm.tsx --logic 'implement progressive profile data capture'

steps.title: Build the Interactive Walkthrough
steps.description: Use a lightweight state machine to guide the user through their first session without overwhelming them with UI clutter.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React state machine configuration to manage a 3-step user onboarding tour that highlights the primary dashboard CTA.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create onboarding state machine and link it to the primary CTA component.'

steps.title: Automate Value Delivery
steps.description: Use backend triggers to send a 'Success' email or notification immediately after the user hits their first milestone.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Node.js function that triggers a webhook to my email provider upon successful completion of the onboarding milestone.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file ./src/lib/onboardingEvents.ts --logic 'trigger success notification on milestone completion'

steps.title: Validate with Analytics
steps.description: Instrument your onboarding flow to track drop-off points. If users aren't finishing the onboarding, your MVP is too complex.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Suggest key metrics to track in my onboarding flow to identify where users drop off and how to optimize for conversion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate analytics hooks into the onboarding components to track funnel conversion.'

pro_tips: Never build an onboarding 'tour' that takes more than 3 steps; if it's longer, your product is too complex.
Use 'empty states' as onboarding tools. If a user has no data, show them a clear, high-contrast button to create their first item.
A/B test your onboarding CTA copy. Changing one word can sometimes double your activation rate overnight.
Force yourself to launch with only the onboarding flow and the core feature; move all 'nice-to-have' features to a post-launch backlog.
Monitor 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) as your primary KPI. Lowering this metric is the fastest way to increase SaaS MRR.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, run 5 user tests with real potential customers. Use their feedback to prune your product roadmap, then commit to shipping the core feature within the next 14 days.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the methodology to stop over-engineering and start collecting revenue. Don't let another project stall in the 'almost finished' phase. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
